brief.topic: the story of the Texas flag — when it was made, what each color means (red for bravery, white for purity, blue for loyalty), and the lone star

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: The Texas Flag
passageRow.body:
## The Texas Flag

The Texas flag is special. It has three colors: red, white, and blue. Each color has a meaning. Red means bravery. White means purity. Blue means loyalty.

The Texas flag has one star. It is called the Lone Star. The star stands for Texas being strong and independent. People in Texas love their Lone Star.

The Texas flag was made in 1839. It has been the same for many years. Texans are proud of their flag. They fly it high in the sky. It is a symbol of Texas.
passageRow.topic: the story of the Texas flag — when it was made, what each color means (red for bravery, white for purity, blue for loyalty), and the lone star
passageRow.topicNotes: Explains the Texas flag's colors and the Lone Star symbol.

questionRows.explanation: Red means bravery. (Line 3)

questionRows.explanation: It is called the Lone Star. (Line 6)

questionRows.explanation: The Texas flag has one star. (Line 5)

brief.topic: where Texas is on a map of the United States — Texas is in the south, touches Mexico, and has the Gulf of Mexico on its east side

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Where Is Texas?
passageRow.body:
## Where Is Texas?

Texas is in the south of the United States. It is a big state. If you look at a map, you can find it near the bottom.

Texas touches Mexico. Mexico is another country. It is to the southwest of Texas. The border between Texas and Mexico is long.

On the east side of Texas, there is the Gulf of Mexico. The gulf is a large body of water. It is part of the ocean. Many people like to visit the beaches there. So, Texas has both land and water around it.
passageRow.topic: where Texas is on a map of the United States — Texas is in the south, touches Mexico, and has the Gulf of Mexico on its east side
passageRow.topicNotes: Teach kids where Texas is located on a map.

questionRows.explanation: Texas is in the south of the United States.

brief.topic: three big Texas cities a first-grader should know — Austin (the capital), Houston (the biggest), and Dallas — and what each one is famous for

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Three Big Texas Cities
passageRow.body:
## Three Big Texas Cities

Texas has many big cities. Three important ones are Austin, Houston, and Dallas. Each city is special in its own way.

Austin is the capital of Texas. The state government works there. Austin is also famous for music. Many people visit to hear bands play.

Houston is the biggest city in Texas. It has a busy port and many tall buildings. Houston is known for space science. NASA is there, where astronauts train to go to space.

Dallas is another big city. It is famous for shopping and sports. Many people like to watch the Dallas Cowboys play football. Each city has its own fun things to do and see!
passageRow.topic: three big Texas cities a first-grader should know — Austin (the capital), Houston (the biggest), and Dallas — and what each one is famous for
passageRow.topicNotes: Geography basics: three major Texas cities and their key features

brief.topic: needs are things we must have to live (food, water, shelter, clothes) and wants are things we like but do not need (toys, candy) — telling them apart

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Needs and Wants
passageRow.body:
## Needs and Wants

We have needs and wants. Needs are things we must have to live. We need food to eat and water to drink. We need clothes to keep us warm. We need a home to live in. These are all important needs.

Wants are different. Wants are things we like but do not need. Toys are fun, and candy is sweet, but we can live without them. We can play with toys, but they are not like food or water. It is fun to have wants, but we must take care of our needs first.

Knowing needs and wants helps us make good choices. We make sure to have what we need to be happy and healthy.
passageRow.topic: needs are things we must have to live (food, water, shelter, clothes) and wants are things we like but do not need (toys, candy) — telling them apart
passageRow.topicNotes: Explain the difference between needs and wants in simple terms.

questionRows.explanation: We must take care of our needs first.

brief.topic: three jobs people do in a Texas town — farmer (grows food), nurse (helps sick people), bus driver (takes kids to school) — and how each helps everyone

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Jobs in a Texas Town
passageRow.body:
## Jobs in a Texas Town

In a Texas town, people do many jobs. Farmers, nurses, and bus drivers help everyone. Each job is important.

Farmers grow food. They plant seeds and take care of crops. They make sure we have fruits and vegetables to eat. Without farmers, we would not have fresh food.

Nurses help people stay healthy. They work in clinics and hospitals. When someone is sick, a nurse gives care and medicine. Nurses help us feel better and stay strong.

Bus drivers take kids to school. They drive big yellow buses. Every morning, they pick up kids and bring them to school safely. Bus drivers help kids learn by getting them to school on time.

These jobs make our town a better place. Farmers, nurses, and bus drivers all help in special ways.
passageRow.topic: three jobs people do in a Texas town — farmer (grows food), nurse (helps sick people), bus driver (takes kids to school) — and how each helps everyone
passageRow.topicNotes: Explains three jobs in a Texas town and their importance.

brief.topic: a mayor is the leader of a city — the mayor helps make rules, fixes streets, and listens to the people who live there

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: What Does a Mayor Do?
passageRow.body:
## What Does a Mayor Do?

A mayor is the leader of a city. The mayor helps keep the city nice and safe. The mayor works in a big office at the city hall. This is where many important meetings happen.

The mayor helps make rules for the city. These rules keep everyone safe and happy. The mayor also helps fix streets and parks. If a road has a hole, the mayor makes sure it gets fixed.

The mayor listens to the people who live in the city. People can talk to the mayor about their ideas and problems. The mayor tries to help solve them. The mayor's job is to take care of the city and its people.
passageRow.topic: a mayor is the leader of a city — the mayor helps make rules, fixes streets, and listens to the people who live there
passageRow.topicNotes: Explain the role of a mayor in simple terms for Grade 1 students.

brief.topic: how things used to be different — long ago people rode horses instead of cars, washed clothes by hand, and lit lamps with fire — now we have cars, washing machines, and electric lights

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: How Things Used to Be Different
passageRow.body:
## How Things Used to Be Different

Long ago, people traveled in different ways. They rode horses to go places. Now, we use cars. Cars are faster and take us far.

In the past, washing clothes was hard work. People washed clothes by hand in a tub. Today, we have washing machines. They help make clothes clean quickly.

Long ago, homes were lit with lamps. These lamps used fire. Today, we have electric lights. We turn them on with a switch. Life is easier now with these changes.
passageRow.topic: how things used to be different — long ago people rode horses instead of cars, washed clothes by hand, and lit lamps with fire — now we have cars, washing machines, and electric lights
passageRow.topicNotes: Comparing past and present methods of travel, cleaning, and lighting.

brief.topic: Texas is one of fifty states in the United States — a state is a piece of a country, and our country is the United States of America

passageRow.subject: social-studies
passageRow.title: Texas and the United States
passageRow.body:
## Texas and the United States

Texas is one of fifty states. Each state is a piece of a country. Our country is the United States of America. That is a long name! We often just say "the U.S.A."

Texas is very big. It is the second largest state. Only Alaska is bigger. Texas has many cities and towns. People from all over live here.

The U.S.A. is our home. Each state is important. Together, they make one big country. We can visit other states. We can learn about them, too. This helps us understand our big world.
passageRow.topic: Texas is one of fifty states in the United States — a state is a piece of a country, and our country is the United States of America
passageRow.topicNotes: Introduce Texas as part of the United States.
